meaning: n.枯萎病,不良影响,打击vt.破坏,使枯萎
examples: All you can do to prevent potato blight is keep an eye on your crops.
An evil person is a blight in the orchard.
The aim is partly aesthetic, but also an attempt to increase the value of neighboring homes by replacing signs of blight with greenery.
Ponting said the referral of disputed catches to inadequate technology was a "blight on the game".
"It's time to declare war on this blight, " says Alan Bradley, London's "street environment" czar.
